intro: Dendrocacalia crepidifolia, also known as the Japanese field-aster, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its flowering stems and leaves that resemble those of the common dandelion. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate various health issues, including its purported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
